thig13.1:1.1: Black was my hair —the color of bees—
thig13.1:1.3: with age, it looked like coarse hemp.
thig13.1:2.3: With age it smelled musty, like animal fur.
thig13.1:3.1: Thick & lush, like a well-tended grove,
thig13.1:3.2: made splendid, the tips elaborate with comb & pin.
thig13.1:3.3: With age, it grew thin & bald here & there.
thig13.1:4.2: it was splendid, ornamented with braids.
thig13.1:4.3: Now, with age, that head has gone bald.
thig13.1:5.1: Curved, as if well-drawn by an artist,
thig13.1:5.3: With age, they droop down in folds.
thig13.1:6.1: Radiant, brilliant like jewels, my eyes:
thig13.1:6.3: With age, they’re no longer splendid.
thig13.1:7.1: Like a delicate peak, my nose
thig13.1:7.2: was splendid in the prime of my youth.
thig13.1:7.3: With age, it’s like a long pepper.
thig13.1:8.3: With age, they droop down in folds.
thig13.1:9.1: Like plantain buds in their color,
thig13.1:10.1: Like that of a cuckoo in the dense jungle, flitting through deep forest thickets:
thig13.1:10.2: sweet was the tone of my voice.
thig13.1:10.3: With age, it cracks here & there.
thig13.1:11.3: With age, it’s broken down, bent.
thig13.1:12.3: With age, they’re like dried up pāṭalī trees.
thig13.1:13.3: With age, they’re like onions & tubers.
thig13.1:14.2: both my breasts were once splendid.
thig13.1:14.3: In the drought of old age, they dangle like empty old water bags.
thig13.1:15.1: Like a sheet of gold, well-burnished,
thig13.1:15.3: Now it’s covered with very fine wrinkles.
thig13.1:16.1: Smooth in their lines, like an elephant’s trunk,
thig13.1:16.2: both my thighs were once splendid.
thig13.1:16.3: With age, they’re like knotted bamboo.
thig13.1:17.3: With age, they’re like sesame sticks.
thig13.1:18.1: As if they were stuffed with soft cotton,
thig13.1:18.2: both my feet were once splendid.
thig13.1:19.2:
now:
decrepit, the home of pains, many pains.
thig13.1:19.3: A house with its plaster all fallen off.
